question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user inject the environment variable HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_BALANCE_ENABLED=true via a misconfigured container/pod spec to flip the default enabled=false to true, causing the balance downloader to activate and begin pulling balance files from cloud storage, leading to unintended data ingestion and increased cloud egress costs?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setFrequency(Duration)] Can an unprivileged external user supply the property hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.balance.frequency=1ms via an externally-readable config source (e.g., a mounted ConfigMap with world-write permissions) to set frequency to an arbitrarily small Duration, bypassing the absence of a @DurationMin constraint on this field, causing the scheduler to spin at near-zero intervals and exhaust CPU and cloud API rate limits?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setFrequency(Duration)] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.balance.frequency to a negative Duration value (e.g., PT-1S), which passes the @NotNull check but has no @DurationMin guard unlike CommonDownloaderProperties fields, causing Duration.toMillis() to return a negative value that is passed to @Scheduled(fixedDelayString), leading to a scheduler exception or undefined polling behavior?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setFrequency(Duration)] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.balance.frequency=PT0S (zero duration), bypassing the missing @DurationMin(seconds=1) constraint present on sibling properties in CommonDownloaderProperties, causing the @Scheduled fixedDelayString SpEL expression to evaluate to 0ms and trigger a tight polling loop that causes a denial-of-service on the cloud storage backend?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the @Scheduled annotation in AccountBalancesDownloader evaluates #{@balanceDownloaderProperties.getFrequency().toMillis()} as a SpEL expression at startup, and by injecting a crafted frequency string that is not a valid ISO-8601 duration via an environment variable, cause a ConversionFailedException at startup that prevents the entire importer application from starting, resulting in a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setPersistBytes(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.balance.persistBytes=true via an environment variable, causing raw balance file bytes to be persisted to the database, leading to unbounded database storage growth and potential disk exhaustion if large or repeated balance files are downloaded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setWriteFiles(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.balance.writeFiles=true via an environment variable, causing verified balance stream files to be written to the filesystem at the path governed by importerProperties.getDataPath(), and if that path is world-readable, allowing the attacker to read sensitive balance data from the filesystem?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setWriteSignatures(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.balance.writeSignatures=true via an environment variable, causing verified signature files to be written to the local filesystem, and if the filesystem path is shared or world-readable, allowing the attacker to harvest node signature material for offline analysis or forgery attempts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getStreamType() is hardcoded to StreamType.BALANCE with no runtime override, and by manipulating the common downloader bucket path prefix (hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.pathPrefix) via environment variable, cause the balance downloader to resolve files from an attacker-controlled path within the bucket, leading to ingestion of malicious balance data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the shared CommonDownloaderProperties bean (e.g., by setting hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.bucketName to an attacker-controlled bucket name via environment variable), which is injected into BalanceDownloaderProperties via getCommon(), causing the balance downloader to pull files from an attacker-controlled cloud storage bucket and ingest forged balance data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that enabled=false is the default but AccountBalancesDownloader.shouldDownload() returns true even when enabled=false if no balance file exists in the database, meaning the downloader runs regardless of the enabled flag on a fresh deployment, and by poisoning the cloud storage bucket with a malformed balance file, cause a parse error that crashes the importer on first boot?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user who can write to a Kubernetes ConfigMap or environment variable set enabled=true while HistoricalBalanceProperties also has enabled=true, triggering the IllegalArgumentException in HistoricalBalanceProperties.init() that prevents the Spring context from starting, causing a denial-of-service by crashing the importer at startup?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setFrequency(Duration)] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.balance.frequency to an extremely large Duration (e.g., PT999999999H), causing the scheduler to effectively never poll for balance files, silently halting balance data ingestion without any error or alert, leading to stale balance data being served to API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setFrequency(Duration)] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.balance.frequency=null via a property source that explicitly sets the value to empty/null, violating the @NotNull constraint and causing a ConstraintViolationException during Spring context initialization, resulting in a denial-of-service by preventing the application from starting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a lower-bound duration constraint on frequency (unlike CommonDownloaderProperties.pathRefreshInterval which has @DurationMin(seconds=1)) to set frequency=PT0.001S, causing the balance downloader to poll cloud storage thousands of times per second, exhausting cloud API quotas and incurring significant financial cost?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setWriteFiles(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user set writeFiles=true and also control the dataPath via hiero.mirror.importer.dataPath to a sensitive filesystem location (e.g., /etc or /var/run), causing verified balance stream files to be written to a sensitive directory, potentially overwriting system files or creating files in unexpected locations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setWriteSignatures(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user set writeSignatures=true and dataPath to a world-readable directory, causing signature files containing node cryptographic signatures to be written to disk and exposed to any local user, enabling offline analysis of signature patterns or replay attacks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setPersistBytes(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user set persistBytes=true and simultaneously set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.maxSize to a very large value via environment variable, causing the balance downloader to download and persist extremely large balance files as raw bytes to the database, leading to database storage exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.allowAnonymousAccess=true via environment variable, which is read through getCommon().isAnonymousCredentials(), causing the balance downloader to use anonymous cloud storage credentials and potentially access or be redirected to a public bucket containing attacker-controlled balance files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.endpointOverride to an attacker-controlled HTTP server via environment variable, which is consumed through getCommon() by the balance downloader's S3 client, causing all balance file download requests to be redirected to the attacker's server, enabling man-in-the-middle injection of forged balance data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user toggle enabled between true and false at runtime via Spring Boot Actuator's /actuator/env endpoint (if exposed without authentication), causing the balance downloader to start and stop unpredictably, leading to gaps in balance data ingestion or race conditions in the shouldDownload() logic?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setFrequency(Duration)] Can an unprivileged external user update frequency at runtime via Spring Boot Actuator's /actuator/env endpoint (if exposed), but since @Scheduled fixedDelayString is evaluated only at startup via SpEL, the runtime change has no effect on the scheduler, creating a false sense of security where an operator believes they have slowed down polling but the original frequency persists?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getStreamType() returns StreamType.BALANCE which is used to construct the cloud storage path, and by manipulating hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.pathPrefix to include path traversal sequences (e.g., ../../), cause the downloader to resolve balance files from an unintended bucket path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.cloudProvider=LOCAL via environment variable, which is read through getCommon().getCloudProvider(), causing the balance downloader to switch from S3/GCP to a local filesystem provider and read balance files from an attacker-controlled local directory, bypassing cloud-based signature verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setWriteFiles(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user set writeFiles=true via environment variable, causing the balance downloader to write files to disk on every successful verification, and if the disk is nearly full, trigger an IOException that causes the importer to crash or enter an error loop, resulting in a denial-of-service?
